purpose: Guide AI in consistently translating client intake data, lab results, and symptom patterns into clear, educational, client-facing protocol
core_rules: Do not invent labs or symptoms
Every lab finding must map to action

estrogen_low.interpretation: Low estrogen may be influenced by lifestyle factors, supplements (e.g., DIM), stress, or lack of ovulation
estrogen_low.why_matters: Estrogen supports mood, libido, sleep quality, and healthy menstrual flow. Low levels can affect how other hormones function
protocol: Emphasize balanced nutrition and stress reduction

progesterone_low.interpretation: Low progesterone is often consistent with anovulatory cycles or luteal phase disruption
progesterone_low.why_matters: Progesterone helps calm the nervous system, support sleep, reduce cramping, and balance estrogen
protocol: Support stress resilience and blood sugar balance

androgen_dominance.interpretation: Elevated androgens relative to estrogen/progesterone may be consistent with PCOS physiology
androgen_dominance.why_matters: High androgens can suppress ovulation, lower progesterone production, and contribute to hormonal imbalance
protocol: Blood sugar regulation is a top priority

methylation_low.interpretation: Reduced methylation capacity may affect hormone clearance and neurotransmitter balance
methylation_low.why_matters: Methylation helps clear hormones, regulate mood, and support detoxification

phase2_estrogen_slow.interpretation: Sluggish estrogen packaging for elimination, even when total estrogen is low
phase2_estrogen_slow.why_matters: Poor Phase 2 detox can lead to estrogen recirculation and hormonal discomfort

cortisol_metabolism_low.interpretation: Cortisol production may be present, but breakdown and clearance are impaired
cortisol_metabolism_low.why_matters: Poor cortisol metabolism disrupts sleep-wake rhythms, stress tolerance, and metabolic health

glutathione_low.interpretation: Reduced antioxidant capacity and detox support
glutathione_low.why_matters: Glutathione is the body's primary antioxidant and supports hormone clearance

b6_deficiency.interpretation: Functional B6 deficiency may impair progesterone production and neurotransmitter balance
b6_deficiency.why_matters: B6 is required for hormone metabolism, mood regulation, and sleep quality
